title: Hey all, I'm a developer who's been building a fully autonomous trading system for Bitcoin prediction markets. After months of coding, testing, and refining, I have something that actually works - and I'm looking to connect with others building their own bot just to discuss.
author: Quant3dge_Labs
selftext:
\*\*What it does\*\*

The bot runs 24/7 on a cloud server with zero manual intervention. It trades short-duration BTC binary contracts, entering positions only when a proprietary probability model identifies a near-certain outcome. It enters, waits minutes, and collects the payout at settlement. It does this dozens of times per day and sends me real-time alerts on my phone for every trade.

\*\*What's under the hood\*\*

\- 13 independent trading systems running in parallel, each exploiting a different edge

\- A self-learning risk management layer that analyzes every completed trade and auto-adjusts sizing, entry thresholds, and strategy parameters in real time

\- Volatility detection that shifts the entire system between aggressive and defensive modes based on live BTC conditions

\- Emergency kill switches, position limits, and drawdown protection baked into every level

\- 36,000+ lines of Python, fully custom, no third-party trading libraries

\*\*Performance\*\*

\- 130+ autonomously executed trades(I’m currently past 300 executed trades with over 90% win rate)

\- 94% overall win rate

\- Primary system running at 97% accuracy

\- Full trade log with entry logic, probability scores, and outcomes for every position

\*\*Why I'm posting\*\*

The system works. The edge is mathematical and repeatable. The bottleneck is capital. These markets have a compounding structure where a larger starting balance leads to significantly faster growth. I'm looking for individuals with similar bots so we can connect

\*\*What I'll share:\*\* Verified trade logs, live demo of the bot running, high-level strategy discussion.

\*\*What I won't share:\*\* The probability model, source code, specific markets, entry timing, or execution logic. I've invested months of work and this is proprietary.

If you're interested, DM me. Serious inquiries only — I'm happy to answer questions and prove the system is real.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: I just asked to discuss about other people bots. How am I scamming?

replies.author: strat-run
replies.body:
It's the whole "DM me" thing. Not being willing to share the source is fine but trying to get people to DM you so they can see how great your bot is just screams of a sales pitch.

If you really want to share then why not just share? Make a YouTube video or something.

Scammers try to push people into non-public conversations so they can't be called out.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Fair. I understand what your saying. Lmao ChatGPT wrote that because I don’t really know how Reddit works n how to connect with others on here, so I just asked it to write something.

replies.author: thawizard
replies.body: Reddit is a place to discuss, not to connect. If your bot works why would you need to connect with desperate people who have no idea how any of this works? I know how this works, I’m building my own shit, I don’t need to connect with you. I’m here to discuss with like-minded individuals, not to network.

comments.author: gfarwell
comments.body: Hey I’ve been building a bitcoin trading system all with AI, happy to connect!

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Will respond once I’m back at my computer!

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Nice — always good to meet other builders in this space. What markets are you focused on? Happy to swap notes at a high level. Feel free to DM me.

comments.author: Opening-Berry-6041
comments.body: dude your risk management layer sounds insane how did you even start thinking about making it self-learning like that?

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Yeah, it’s actually really interesting to see it work in real time. I’ll come back to this with a better response once I’m at my computer.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Regarding the self learning - The system logs every trade — entry conditions, probability score, outcome, market state at the time. After each trade closes, the risk layer analyzes what worked and what didn't, then adjusts parameters like position sizing and entry thresholds. It's not machine learning in the deep learning sense — it's adaptive calibration based on its own live performance data. The system logs every trade — entry conditions, probability score, outcome, market state at the time. After each trade closes, the risk layer analyzes what worked and what didn't, then adjusts parameters like position sizing and entry thresholds. It's not machine learning in the deep learning sense — it's adaptive calibration based on its own live performance data.

comments.author: Comfortable-Tank9270
comments.body:
Actually yes - why not do discuss it here? Have also building my own bot, but without AI - pure algo using java with multiple microservice etc, not sticked to BTC only, have about 20 base strategies and a set of 'substrategies', so at all about 200 different strategies used, all this using multiple-way filtering scan system (to choose 'best' pairs to trade today, int this hour, etc.), backtesting services etc.

But all this is useless until you can prove it, for example, on a demo account with concrete trading results for a month (months). For now - I can't :D

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Hey, I’ll come back to this when I’m at my computer . I’d be holy to chat more

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body:
Appreciate the transparency on your setup — 200 strategies across multiple pairs is no joke. Respect the work.

On the proof side, I hear you. I've got 130+ live trades logged with full entry logic, probability scores, and outcomes for each one. Not a demo account — real money, real execution. Happy to share the logs privately with anyone serious.

I keep the implementation details close because that's the IP, but I'm always down to talk high-level architecture and compare notes. What's your win rate looking like across those 200 strategies?

replies.author: epicskyes
replies.body: 200 eh? Do you count gears like a bike and multiply 30s x 5rs?

replies.author: Comfortable-Tank9270
replies.body: As I wrote above, this totals to about 200 strategies plus substrategies. Substrategies are, for example, a base strategy plus a filter or a group of filters, or some condition depending on another strategy, etc. Therefore, some substrategies are practically identical, while others are significantly differs from their base.

replies.author: epicskyes
replies.body: 🤔practically identical you say

replies.author: Comfortable-Tank9270
replies.body:
It's difficult to say what is WR because it completely depends on periods: e.g. since aug 2025 one of my simplest basic strategy shows +1800% growth (but second one just +400%) with about 2500 trades on 5 selected pairs (using "selection" approach for each day), but 95% of that grows periods is about 5 days across all this periods that fires on altcoins, but first half of 2025y the same strategy has continious down trend of losses with about -500% after 6 months to 07.2025.
It simply to show in the chart at all.
So for now implementing approach switch strategies between these periods and at all select when "lucky" period is actually ending.

comments.author: Environmental_Two581
comments.body: Did you test this with real money?

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Yes, for this specific model I just started with 25$ with dynamic tier based sizing per trade just to see where it would take it. By the end of the day it made ~15$

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: I also did a more aggressive model(not recommended as the loss was a lot). that made around ~800 with starting capitol being ~300 before it lost. At the end of the day I was over ~150$ in profit though.

replies.body: Dm you

comments.author: Toastti
comments.body: Yum, python, a language that is not even type safe performing financial calculations. Sounds like a winning combination /s

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Half of Wall Street's quant infrastructure runs on Python. Jane Street is the exception, not the rule. But fair point — I use Decimal for all financial math, not floats. Type safety isn't the same as numerical precision.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body:
Fair skepticism — I'd think the same thing if I saw this post cold. That's why I'm offering to show verified trade logs and do a live screen share of the bot running in real time. I'm not asking anyone to send money to a wallet. I'm looking for a partner who wants to verify everything first.

Happy to prove it to anyone willing to look.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Fair skepticism — I'd think the same thing if I saw this post cold. I'm not asking anyone for money. Just sharing what I've built and connecting with other people in the algo trading space. Happy to show trade logs or do a live demo for anyone curious.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body: Just discussion. And maybe helping others with their bot if I can :).

replies.author: Curiosity-1
replies.body:
Okay awesome well I have no capital anyways ha. But I might soon -> go look at NetList. Not investment advice do your own due diligence. 

But I am interesting in learning algo / agent trading. I don’t have a developer background so the learning curve here is a steep one. More of a curiosity that I want to build on over time… so 

What would you recommend as foundational groundwork. There’s a lot of how to’s and I’ll show you how to build but I know enough to know I don’t know enough to judge the quality of what I’ll learn from one vs another

That’s not to say I shouldn’t start somewhere. So I probably will soon and just get my hands dirty. 

Any architecture you can provide to the learning curve, and possibly explanations of why, would be incredible 

Such as, start with A because ___ is incredibly important and it’s a common source of error/misunderstanding 
The B because _ and pay attention _ 
…
Etc

Thank you, sincerely, for the time to respond. I have confidence from your post I can learn a lot from the architecture you might provide

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body:
Honestly, start with fundamentals first: understand probability, expected value, risk management concepts. Then pick a market and build simple. Don't try to build 13 systems on day one.

The learning curve is steep but worth it. The architecture stuff comes later — first prove you can make money with one clean, simple strategy.

Happy to point you in a direction if you want to chat.

replies.author: Curiosity-1
replies.body: Thank you for offering man (or woman). I’ll send you a dm tonight or tomorrow night - got a 6mo at home but will open dialogue soon. I’m analytically inclined (studied math undergrad years ago, getting closer to 2 decades than one) and have a good innate understanding but have poor proficiency with modeling. Never applied the math; retained creative problem solving mentality, but no coding or even excel background. Very excited for AI because of this, and have tried building macro and micro NI / EPS / MC projections and DCF and some other confidence-level scenario (kind of risk mgmt analogous)   Projects but the AI has such poor memory right now I have to use it to create code for what I want to do. Which is very interesting, but daunting. For how powerful today’s tools are, when you don’t know what you don’t know / what questions to ask, they can’t help you. Thanks again chat soon

comments.author: eggrally
comments.body: In 6 months , bots will control the market,  bot vs bot,  I told ai , to build me a bot that makes money, it went out and did it,  how long the edge will last,  no one knows.

comments.author: schitzblythe
comments.body: Sounds interesting but full automation is always tricky. Market shifts fast and bots do not always react well. Some traders lean toward prospero ai style signals instead so they can still step in when needed. That balance feels safer

comments.author: El_Dyrol
comments.body: Very interesting. You’re building the exact same thing I have been working on for the past month. I’m very interested to hear how you got yours to be profitable, dynamic, and fully autonomous within only 300 trades. Mine had its first full profitable day this week before a bug wiped everything out.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body:
That's solid progress — first profitable day is a real milestone. The bug wipe is rough but that's part of the grind.

On the 300 trades thing — honestly it's a mix of: (1) starting with a solid probability model instead of curve-fitting, (2) aggressive risk management from day one (kill switches, position limits), and (3) running multiple systems so if one underperforms, the others carry the load.

What was the bug that wiped it? Always interested to hear what catches people. Happy to compare notes.

comments.author: inquisitive_swimmer
comments.body:
*** Outsider lurking *** a couple of thoughts that immediately come to mind. 

1. Do trading platforms have the ability to detect (and therefore restrict) these bots ? 

2. Do the trading platforms care, as they are making more money due to transaction fees and questionable fills? 

Just curious.

replies.author: Quant3dge_Labs
replies.body:
Great question. Platforms don't restrict bots — they're indifferent. They make money on volume, whether it's human or automated. The only restriction is if you violate their terms (like manipulating markets or using their API in ways they explicitly ban).

The real risk is your edge disappearing if too many people use the same system. That's why the implementation details matter.

comments.author: Emotional-Cod-7507
comments.body: They don’t mind the bots, in fact they in-courage it. They get paid by transaction, they could care less who or what makes them
